title: Monotone-constrained regime model for pit-window features
summary: The best features already encode intuitive pit pressure variables like tyre age vs expected life, finish-tyre deficit, and old-tyre late-race pressure.
rationale: The best features already encode intuitive pit pressure variables like tyre age vs expected life, finish-tyre deficit, and old-tyre late-race pressure. Tree ensembles can still learn jagged local inversions from noise; monotonic constraints are a direct way to stabilize those strategy relationships when the domain direction is clear.
implementation_hint: Train an XGBoost or LightGBM constrained model where probability is non-decreasing in features such as tyre_age_vs_expected, finish_tyre_deficit, laps_remaining_vs_expected_life, and perhaps pit-window pressure, while constraining obviously inverse features if justified. Use it as either a standalone challenger or a low-weight ensemble member against the unconstrained baseline.
expected_effect: Could improve generalization on rare compounds/races by forcing smoother, more physically plausible pit-window behavior instead of memorizing local quirks.
risk: Wrong constraint signs or over-constraining interaction-heavy features can hurt AUC; some features are only conditionally monotone so constraints should be limited to the clearest ones.
